reference_answer: Quanzhou Wentelai Import and Export Trading Co., Ltd., d/b/a Sperax. (Source: "Description" paragraph: "The importer Quanzhou Wentelai Import and Export Trading Co., Ltd., d/b/a Sperax, has refused to agree to an acceptable recall.")
Date: April 16, 2026. Number: 26-415. (Source: dedicated fields "Product Safety Warning Date" and "Product Safety Warning Number".)
China. (Source: "Manufactured In:" field; also stated inline in the Description: "The products were manufactured in China.")
www.SaferProducts.gov. (Source: end of Description and the final reporting prompt: "report it to CPSC on SaferProducts.gov".)
Black with red trim. (Source: Description: "marketed and sold under the Sperax brand name and are black with red trim".)

In the "Description" section, fourth paragraph after the lead: "The importer Quanzhou Wentelai Import and Export Trading Co., Ltd., d/b/a Sperax, has refused to agree to an acceptable recall." The page does not repeat this statement in any of the labeled summary fields.
The "Manufactured In:" field. (Country also restated within the Description paragraph.)

At the end of the Description section, in a paragraph reading: "Under section 6(b) of the Consumer Product Safety Act, the CPSC is required to include with this press release any comments from the manufacturer or a summary thereof. The company objects to this press release."
In the Description section: "The model number is located on a label near the power switch." It does not appear in any labeled summary field.
The "Product Safety Warning Number:" field.

Pro, Q1, RM-01, RM-02. Source order in the Description: "Affected models include Pro, Q1, RM-01 and RM-02." Note that the verb is "include" — the list is presented as inclusive but the page does not explicitly say it is exhaustive; in practice it should be treated as the closed set since all four affected models are listed and the image carousel shows the same four.

Amazon.com, Walmart.com, NewEgg.com, eBay.com. The introducer is "including," so the list is illustrative, not closed; other platforms may exist. Same list appears twice on the page — once inside the Description paragraph and once in the "Sold At:" labeled field.
Carousel order: Q1, Pro, RM-02, RM-01. Description order: Pro, Q1, RM-01, RM-02. The two orderings differ; neither is canonical. The set is the same in both: {Pro, Q1, RM-01, RM-02}.

(a) Qumeney Bicycle Light Sets Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Battery Ingestion; Violates Mandatory Standard for Consumer Products with Button Cell Batteries; Sold on Amazon by Smfanlus. (b) American Honda Motor Recalls Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death Due to Crash Hazard. (c) Acer America Corporation Recalls Two-Wheeled Folding Electric Scooters Due to Risk of Injury from Fall Hazard. (d) Earthtec Pool Drain Covers Recalled Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Entrapment and Drowning Hazards; Violates Virginia Graeme Baker Pool & Spa Safety Act; Manufactured by Tonyidea.

Category 1 — Stability-related failures: 201 reports of uncontrollable speed, abrupt stops and similar stability-related failures; at least 66 falls or injuries; at least one concussion that required medical attention. Category 2 — Thermal incidents: 573 reports of overheating, fires and other thermal incidents; four reports of minor burns. (Source: Description paragraphs 2 and 3.) Both "at least" qualifiers in Category 1 indicate lower bounds; the "four" figure in Category 2 is given without an "at least" qualifier.

None. The page states only that consumers should "stop using the walking pads and treadmills immediately and dispose of them" and "Do not sell or give away these hazardous products." No refund, no repair, no replacement, no contact information for the firm. This is consistent with the firm having refused to agree to an acceptable recall.

No. The page says only "has refused to agree to an acceptable recall" without a date. The page also does not state whether negotiations took place, how many recall proposals were rejected, or whether the refusal is final or ongoing.

The page distinguishes the two by the source's URL path (`/Warnings/2026/...` rather than `/Recalls/2026/...`) and by its labeled fields ("Product Safety Warning Date," "Product Safety Warning Number"). The implied distinction, given the firm's refusal of an acceptable recall, is that this is a unilateral CPSC public warning rather than a voluntary or Commission-ordered recall coordinated with the firm. The page reinforces this with: "Federal law prohibits any person from selling products subject to a Commission ordered recall or a voluntary recall undertaken in consultation with the CPSC." — a passage that describes the two recall types neither of which describes this action.
No. The page does not report a unit count, a price, manufacturing date ranges, or sales date ranges. These are typically present on CPSC recall pages but are missing here.

"CPSC urges consumers to stop using the walking pads and treadmills immediately and dispose of them. Do not sell or give away these hazardous products." (Source: stated twice on the page, first in the "Consumer Action:" field at the top and again in bold near the end of the Description.) The directive contains four sub-actions: (1) stop using; (2) immediately; (3) dispose; (4) do not sell or give away.

The page summarizes Section 6(b) as: "the CPSC is required to include with this press release any comments from the manufacturer or a summary thereof." CPSC fulfilled this requirement by including the statement "The company objects to this press release." That single sentence is the entirety of the manufacturer's comment as represented on this page. CPSC did not include any longer comment or paraphrase of the firm's substantive objections.

At least 66 falls or injuries. The page names "at least one concussion that required medical attention" as a specific injury. Both quantifications are lower bounds ("at least"). The page does not describe other specific injury types among the 66.

No. The "Incidents/Injuries:" labeled field repeats only the thermal-incident numbers (573 reports of overheating/fires/thermal incidents and four reports of minor burns). It does NOT include the stability-failure numbers (201 reports, 66 falls/injuries, at least one concussion). Those stability numbers appear only in the Description paragraph. A consumer or extractor reading only the "Incidents/Injuries" field would miss the stability-failure totals entirely.

Neither ordering is canonical on the face of the page. The Description orders them Pro → Q1 → RM-01 → RM-02; the carousel orders them Q1 → Pro → RM-02 → RM-01. The union is the same four-element set: {Pro, Q1, RM-01, RM-02}. Both orderings are presentational; the canonical extracted set should be the unordered set of four model designations.

No on all four. A typical CPSC Recall page has labeled "Units," "Sold At" with date ranges and prices, "Recall Date," "Recall Number," and consumer remedy (refund/repair/replacement) with contact information for the recalling firm. This Warning page has "Product Safety Warning Date" and "Product Safety Warning Number" in place of the recall date and recall number; "Manufactured In:" but no manufacturing date range; "Sold At:" with platforms but no date ranges or prices; no Units field; no remedy contact for the firm. The absence is structural and reflects the fact that the firm declined to participate in a coordinated recall.
